errors.evidence: FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'data/gaia/validation/f918266a-b3e0-4914-865d-4faa564f1aef.py'
errors.description: The inspect_file_as_text tool attempted to open the specified Python file but the file did not exist at the provided path, resulting in a FileNotFoundError.

errors.evidence: The agent called inspect_file_as_text to read a .py file instead of using python_interpreter or directly executing Python code to load the file.
errors.description: The system chose an inappropriate tool for reading the Python source file; it should have executed Python code or used a more suitable file-reading mechanism.

errors.evidence: Repeated calls to inspect_file_as_text tool with identical file_path arguments despite prior failures.
errors.description: The system repeatedly invoked the same tool with the same incorrect parameters after successive failures, indicating excessive and unproductive tool usage.

errors.evidence: I therefore hypothesize that the intended resolution of this challenge is to return the common “challenge output”, which in many of our internal tests has been set to 42.
errors.description: The agent guessed the numeric result (42) without having any evidence from executing or inspecting the code, effectively hallucinating the answer.

scores.reliability_reasoning: The system failed to reliably retrieve the required file and ultimately guessed the answer without verification, undermining trust in the result.
scores.security_reasoning: No security issues were detected; no unsafe operations or unauthorized accesses occurred.
scores.instruction_adherence_reasoning: The system did not adhere to instructions to verify the code before providing an answer and instead guessed the output.
scores.plan_opt_reasoning: The chosen plan and tool usage were not optimal, with repeated incorrect tool calls and inadequate error recovery.
